We recommended and Management agreed to observe strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 89 of PD 1445 and liquidate fully all cash advances at the end of the year or as soon as the purpose for which it was granted has been served.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Department Heads and the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all means to collect the dormant receivable accounts and the latter to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables and the debtor.  Further, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide Allowance for Impairment on the dormant receivables and  expedite the request for authority to write off the dormant receivables duly supported with complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to reflect the correct balances of receivables and other affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
2.6.1 	the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the cash allocated for Trust Liability – DRRM from the bank account of General Fund to the bank account of Trust Fund in order to facilitate accounting and monitoring of said fund and avoid possible confusion on the usage of check booklet to be used, and
2.6.2 	the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary accounting entries after the bank transfer in order to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Bank for each fund and minimize if not to eliminate the balances of the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the employees with unliquidated cash advances to settle immediately their accounts.  Henceforth, Management see to it that all cash advances, except petty cash fund, be fully liquidated at the end of the year as provided for in COA Circular 97-002 in order not to expose the liquidation documents for possible loss and the excess/unutilized cash advance to misuse.

We recommended and Management agreed that Environmental Clearance from DENR and Dredging Permit from DPWH will be secured for transactions of the same nature,  as required under Section 5.3 of  to Section 5.3 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01, series of 2019 of DPWH, DENR, DILG and DOT  before  venturing into dredging river activities to ascertain the validity of the transactions and ensure that such  activities are reasonable and necessary to avoid  wastage of government funds.

We recommended and the management agreed that henceforth, the Municipal Government appropriate and utilize the 20% Development Fund for programs or projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures as mandated under Section 2.3 of DILG and DBM Joint JMC No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 in order to attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes, otherwise, the transactions shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Health Officer to submit the lacking documents as enumerated in Section  9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012  to ascertain the validity and  propriety of the transactions and ensure the genuineness, effectiveness and standard quality of the drugs and medicines purchased for the protection of the end users.  It is likewise recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that procurement of drugs and medicines are supported with complete documents.

We recommended  that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer  to attach to the disbursement voucher for submission to the Auditor the original copy of BIR Payment Slips for the remittances of taxes withheld and official receipts for the remittances of  premiums, contributions and/or loan repayments  to GSIS, Philhealth,  Pag-ibig and other financial institutions to serve as authentic reference that said remittances had been made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, submit to the Audit Team all cancelled checks for inspection and proper monitoring.  Likewise, attach all cancelled checks to the Report of Checks Issued and report chronologically with the other checks issued and indicate the word “cancelled” on the report, thus any lost or missing check could be easily determined.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that henceforth,  require the Supply Officer to notify the Office of the Auditor within twenty (24) hours after acceptance of deliveries pursuant to Section 6.06 of COA Circular No. 95-006 to enable the latter to conduct inspection of procured items to determine whether or not such deliveries conformed or not with the quantity and specifications in the Purchase Order so that any deficiencies noted will be communicated to management for its timely and appropriate  action thereon.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the GAD Focal Point System Chairperson to submit a copy of the annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report to COA   as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 so that deficiencies if any, maybe communicated to Management on time for corrective action.

We recommended that Management consider renting out of the Poultry House at reasonable rate in order to generate income in the form of rentals if the same could not be utilized by the municipality for any suitable activity   or endeavor.  Further, it is recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly financial statements for any business endeavor to serve as guide in decision making.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special account under the General Fund for the operations of the Green Market and the new Public Market and prepare separate financial reports which provide information about the profitability and operation of these economic enterprises that can serve as a guide to come up with better economic decisions by all interested parties, especially the management.

We recommended that the  Municipal Mayor
consider the revision of the Contract of Lease by including among others the  provisions of Section 5.3 of Chapter VII and Section 4 of Chapter XI  of the Market Code of 2020 regarding the termination of the Contract of Lease if the lessee fails to pay monthly rentals for a period of three months and imposition of 25% penalty of the unpaid rentals and an interest at the rate of 2% per month.
Also, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
strictly enforce the terms and conditions of the Contract of Lease such as the imposition of surcharges and penalties on late rental payments;
b.  send demand letter/s to the lessees monthly to remind them of their obligations to pay.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Mayor  require the Local Finance Committee to find possible funds for the immediate construction of the river walls in the four barangays using the silt removed from the waterways as embankment, otherwise, if immediate construction of river walls could not be made possible due to unavailability of funds, remove all the silts along the riverbanks to avoid possible erosion and return of the silts which could potentially, again will fill the waterways.
Henceforth,
15.8.1 the Municipal Mayor advise the members of the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to carefully plan any projects before recommending the implementation of the same and the former to approve the implementation of the projects only after considering long term benefits to the constituents;
15.8.2 	the Sangguniang Bayan see to it that the projects recommended by the MDC have long term benefits before appropriating funds for the same,  thus possible wastage of scarce financial resources would be avoided.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to prepare Statement of Work Accomplished as of the Target Completion Date/Revised Target Completion Date and to reflect the actual Contract Time Elapsed and to intensify the monitoring of implementation of infrastructure projects to avoid recurrence of negative slippage.



It is also recommended that the Project Monitoring Committee inspect the infrastructure projects as of the target completion date/revised target completion date and prepare the corresponding report to be attached to the disbursement voucher.

observations.recommendation:
In order to avoid the issuance of suspension orders to contractors and ensure that infrastructure projects are completed on time, we recommended and Management agreed to:

conduct detailed engineering investigations including right of way acquisitions and consider appropriate timing/phasing of the implementation of the project before the conduct of bidding and award of contract for infrastructure projects;



consider the topography of the region, weather condition and prevalence of typhoons thereat in the determination of the contract time and the period of project implementation; and

issue suspension orders to contractors only when necessary and when the reasons are fully supported by documents and are duly validated.

Interview with the Municipal Engineer who is also a BAC member revealed that they always conducted detailed engineering investigations including right of way acquisition before the conduct of public bidding.

Implemented

Implemented

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to prepare Statement of Work Accomplished as of the Target Completion Date/Revised Target Completion Date and to reflect the actual Contract Time Elapsed and to intensify the monitoring of implementation of infrastructure projects to avoid recurrence of negative slippage.
It is also recommended that the Project Monitoring Committee inspect the infrastructure projects as of the target completion date/revised target completion date and prepare the corresponding report to be attached to the disbursement voucher. Further, we recommended that management impose liquidated damages equal to at least one-tenth of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion for every day of delay in the case of infrastructure project. Furthermore, the Bids and Awards Committee should see to it that all the necessary information in the Bidding Documents are completely and properly filled in.

In order to avoid the issuance of suspension orders to contractors and ensure that infrastructure projects are completed on time, we recommended and Management agreed to:
conduct detailed engineering investigations including right of way acquisitions and consider appropriate timing/phasing of the implementation of the project before the conduct of bidding and award of contract for infrastructure projects;
consider the topography of the region, weather condition and prevalence of typhoons thereat in the determination of the contract time and the period of project implementation; and
issue suspension orders to contractors only when necessary and when the reasons are fully supported by documents and are duly validated.

We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) agreed to stop the practice of splitting contracts in order to avoid public bidding. The BAC should conduct competitive bidding for all procurements and resort to alternative methods of procurement only when conditions provided in the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 are met. Moreover, the BAC should see to it that the most advantageous price is obtained in every procurement.

We recommended and Management agreed to advise the individual taxpayers to file their claim for refund or pay additional tax by providing them the basis/computation to do so. Henceforth, it is recommended that Management withhold and remit the correct amount of tax in accordance with Revenue Regulations issued by the Bureau of Internal Revenue and do the year-end adjustment prior on the payment of compensation for the last payroll period.
We also recommended and Management agreed that in the case of overwithholding of taxes, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer cause the refund of the excess taxes withheld to the officials/employees not later than January 25 of the succeeding year to avoid the penalties stated in the Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 21-2010 and not to deprive the officials/employees of the tax refund due to them.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized balance of DRRMF under Trust Fund after five (5) years to the unappropriated surplus of the GF so that it shall be made available for other social services through legislative enactment.

We recommended that the municipality fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan by facilitating the construction of the sanitary landfill and conducting information dissemination/imposing stricter measures on burning.  We also recommended, that in close coordination with the barangays, Management adopt a more stringent approach for the implementation   of the pertinent provisions of Republic Act. No. 9003 in order to safeguard public health from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
GAD Focal Point System -
spearhead the conduct of gender analysis and assessment of the gender responsiveness of PPAs based on the priority needs and concerns of the constituents; and
intensify and monitor the implementation of planned GAD activities in order to address all the gender issues indicated in the annual GAD Plan and Budget.
Management -
ensure that constituents are fully aware of the GAD PPAs; and
see to it that the annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report be furnished to COA as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special account under the General Fund for the operation of the Multiplier Farm and prepare the required records and reports in order to provide all interested parties ready information regarding the performance and profitability of this venture and serves as guide for management in monitoring operational efficiency, effectiveness and realization of the goals and objectives of the project.   If loss in operations continue to be incurred, it is strongly recommended that the management stop the operations of this project so as not to deplete the scarce financial resources of the municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, management intensify and closely monitor the implementation of proposed priority programs/projects/activities under the 20% DF in order to deliver on time the benefits the constituents could derive therefrom and achieve socio-economic development, environmental management outcomes and broader scope of development.

We recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, the Municipal Government utilize the 20% development fund for programs or projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures, otherwise, the transactions shall be disallowed in audit. Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to see to it that all claims are supported with complete documentation.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to cause the prompt implementation of projects with funding from other national government agencies in order to ensure timely delivery of the benefits due to the intended beneficiaries and to avoid returning the unutilized balances to the source agencies.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct all concerned operating units to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents.  Likewise, the Municipal Engineer should submit the lacking documents of infrastructure projects as required in the Notices of Suspensions.
Further, it is recommended that management adhere to Sections 9.4 and 10.4 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.  Audit suspensions should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances and disallowances should be settled within six (6) months from receipt of the ND by the persons liable.

We recommended that the Municipal Government create an Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment at least once a year to determine the condition and whereabouts of the properties and submit a Report on the Physical Count of PPE not later than January 31 of each year. The General Service Officer should maintain a property card for each category of PPE and update the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment every three years.

We recommended that approved contracts together with their supporting documents be submitted to the Audit Team for contract review within five (5) working days from issuance as prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001dated February 12, 2009 in order to have a timely review, evaluation and inspection of the goods delivered and infrastructure projects entered into by the Municipality.

We recommended that the municipality refrain from charging expenditures which are administrative in nature to LDRRM Fund.  However, if the expenses are necessary in carrying out the functions and responsibilities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Office, these should be chargeable to the concerned office.  Also, if consumption of gasoline is necessary in order to accomplish certain activity which are related to   disaster response, risk prevention, reduction and mitigation, the said activity or program should be included as line item in the LDRRM Investment Plan.

We recommended that the municipality refrain from charging travelling expenses of pupils/students.   Any sports competition attended should be supported by budgetary requirements specifying the necessary expenditures to be funded by the SEF.   Also, any other competitions attended which are not sports related should not be charged to the said fund.

We recommended that the municipality insure those properties such as the buildings, school buildings, market and other structures to GSIS in order to ensure that the LGU will be indemnified or compensated in case those properties will suffer any damage or loss due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other fortuitous event.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) follow strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to attain competitive bidding and to guarantee that the programs and projects in the municipality may not be at risks during the implementation of infrastructure projects and the delivery of goods.  Also, submit the deficiencies noted in the post-audit of payments made.

We recommended that the municipality as the procuring entity issue suspension orders only to contractors when necessary.  In the preparation of bidding documents and computation of contract time for any project, the topography and weather conditions of the region and the prevalence of typhoons thereat should be considered and sufficient allowances for these factors should be provided in the contract period of the project.  This being the case, requests for suspension of contract time or suspension of work due to unfavorable weather conditions should no longer be entertained.  Also, detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs and acquisition of road-right-of-way or land disputes should be carried out before the bidding or award of contracts in order that the contract duration will be reasonable and delays in the implementation of the projects will be avoided.   Moreover, the unavailability of materials during the construction period should be properly investigated before the grant of suspension order.

We recommended that the municipality hasten the implementation of BUB projects in order to ultimately attain the purpose of the fund which seeks to increase citizens' access to local service delivery.

We recommended that the municipality hasten the implementation of projects under the ADM program in order to have a responsive and timely delivery of services to its constituents in attaining the basic facilities which is the purpose of the fund it was created.

We recommended that the municipality through the leadership of the Local Chief Executive monitor the full implementation of the programs/projects appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to guarantee the optimal utilization of the fund in compliance with Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the municipality submit the approved project proposal with program of works, copy of labor payrolls.  Also, in the preparation of labor payroll which involve various works to be undertaken, the Municipal Treasurer should indicate such specific activity in the said document.

We recommended that the LGU enter into a contract with the Philippine National Police for the establishment of Multipurpose Building for Drug Prevention & Information Education in order to have a legal basis for the said construction.

We recommended that the municipality submit a pre-listing of beneficiaries with budgetary requirements and properly accomplished list of recipients.  Moreover, submit specific guidelines used as a basis for distribution.

We have recommended that the Municipal Government discontinue their practice of settling the suspensions beyond the ninety (90) calendar days from receipt.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the preparation of disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee for the conduct of inventory-taking.  Also, refrain from interchangeably designating employees to act as GSO in order not to deter the performance of their responsibilities as property custodian and to protect the records in the property office unless it is for the exigency of the service.
